clusters.reasoning: These keys all refer to the physician's ongoing record of the patient's condition, treatment plan, and observations, which are typically part of the same physical clinical notes document.
This key refers to the comprehensive summary provided upon patient discharge.
These keys all describe the official record of a surgical or interventional procedure, detailing steps, findings, and indications, which are part of the same physical document.
These keys all refer to an electrocardiogram report, which is a single diagnostic test.
These keys refer to multiple ECGs taken over time, which are distinct from a single ECG report.
This is a very specific type of ECG document related to pacemaker implantation, distinct from a general ECG report or serial ECGs.
These keys refer to an echocardiography report, which is a single ultrasound-based diagnostic test, sometimes including still images.
These keys specifically refer to an echocardiography report performed after a procedure, often including stills, which is a distinct post-intervention assessment.

These keys consistently refer to a combined echocardiography and Doppler ultrasound report, often with stills, representing a single diagnostic document combining these two related ultrasound modalities.
These keys refer to a combined echocardiography and Doppler ultrasound report performed after a procedure.
These keys all refer to an angiography report, which is an X-ray based diagnostic test, often including stills.
These keys refer to an angiography report performed after a procedure, often including stills, which is a distinct post-intervention assessment.
These keys represent a bundled report that includes both Colour Doppler and CT Angiography, which are distinct imaging modalities but requested together in this specific document.
These keys represent a bundled report that includes both Doppler and Angiography, which are distinct imaging modalities but requested together in this specific document.
These keys represent a bundled report that includes both Echocardiography and Angiography, which are distinct imaging modalities but requested together in this specific document.

clusters.canonical_label: Post Procedure Echo / Color Doppler / Angio Stills
clusters.reasoning: This key represents a bundled post-procedure document combining stills from Echo, Colour Doppler, and Angiography.

clusters.canonical_label: Per-procedure or Post-procedure Stills Showing Stent
clusters.reasoning: This key represents a specific document showing per- or post-procedure stills from either angiography or echocardiography, specifically demonstrating a stent in place.

clusters.canonical_label: Doppler Ultrasound / Digital Subtraction Angiography / Computed Tomography Angiography / Magnetic Resonance Angiography Report
clusters.reasoning: This key represents a highly bundled diagnostic report encompassing multiple distinct imaging modalities, and must not be merged with individual modality reports.

clusters.canonical_label: Investigation Reports (ECG / ECHO / Cardiac Enzymes / Angiography / Holter)
clusters.reasoning: These keys represent a highly bundled diagnostic report encompassing multiple distinct tests and modalities, and must not be merged with individual test reports.

clusters.canonical_label: Relevant Investigations (Duplex Ultrasound / Angiography / CTA / MRA)
clusters.reasoning: These keys represent a highly bundled diagnostic report encompassing multiple distinct imaging modalities, and must not be merged with individual modality reports.

clusters.canonical_label: Invoice of Catheter and Accessories Used
clusters.reasoning: These keys all refer to the invoice for catheters and related accessories used in a procedure.
These keys all refer to the invoice for thrombolytic drugs or agents used.
These keys all refer to the invoice for a balloon or blade used in a procedure.
This key refers to the invoice for a stent used.
This key explicitly bundles the invoice and barcode for catheters, and must remain separate from pure invoices or pure barcodes.

clusters.canonical_label: Invoice / Barcode of Balloon / Device Used
clusters.reasoning: These keys explicitly bundle the invoice and barcode for balloons, implants, or devices, and must remain separate from pure invoices or pure barcodes.
This key explicitly bundles the invoice and barcode for a stent, and must remain separate from pure invoices or pure barcodes.

clusters.canonical_label: Invoice / Barcode of Pacemaker / Pacing Lead
clusters.reasoning: These keys explicitly bundle the invoice and barcode for a pacemaker or pacing lead, and must remain separate from pure invoices or pure barcodes.
These keys all refer to the barcode or serial number label of an implanted device or graft, which is a distinct physical artifact from an invoice.
These keys refer to laboratory reports for cardiac enzymes, which are the same test regardless of when performed.
This key refers to a standard laboratory report for routine biochemistry tests.
This key refers to a laboratory report detailing the analysis of fluid removed during a procedure.
This key refers to an Electrophysiology (EP) study report, a distinct diagnostic procedure.
This key refers to a standard chest X-ray imaging report.
This key refers to a High-Resolution CT of the Chest or CT Pulmonary Angiography, which are specific CT imaging modalities.
This key refers to a specific X-ray image documenting the placement of a pacemaker.

clusters.canonical_label: Post-Op Chest X-ray Showing Sternal Sutures
clusters.reasoning: This key refers to a specific post-operative chest X-ray focused on sternal sutures.
These keys refer to photographic documentation of the patient, either general or during a procedure.
